headline: Tanglin Trust retains Outstanding grade in every BSO inspection category
dek: Singapore's oldest British school has again been awarded the highest possible rating across all areas by British Schools Overseas inspectors, cementing its position as the only international school in the city-state subject to the UK government framework.
author: marcus-chen

body.text: Tanglin Trust School has received an Outstanding rating in every category following its most recent British Schools Overseas (BSO) inspection, carried out in November 2025. The school is the only international school in Singapore to undergo the UK government-recognised inspection scheme, and the result marks a consecutive Outstanding across quality of education, students' personal development and behaviour, welfare and safety, and leadership and management.

Inspectors visited 122 lessons and held 51 meetings with leaders, teachers, pupils and parents during the inspection, according to the published report by the Education Development Trust, which administers BSO inspections on behalf of the UK government. The school was found to meet all BSO standards applicable in the host country.

The inspection report highlighted the school's "exceptional curriculum" and described teaching, learning and assessment as "consistently outstanding." Inspectors noted that relationships across the school were "warm, respectful and productive," and described pupils as "highly motivated" and demonstrating "high levels of engagement and focus." Safeguarding procedures were also described as "very robust."

Founded in 1925, Tanglin Trust is the oldest British international school in Southeast Asia and operates on a not-for-profit basis, with tuition fees directed back into educational provision. The school enrols approximately 2,800 students from more than 50 nationalities, spanning Nursery through to Sixth Form. It is the only school in Singapore to offer pupils a choice between A Levels and the IB Diploma in Sixth Form.

The BSO result follows strong IB Diploma outcomes in 2025, when the school's cohort achieved a 100 per cent pass rate with an average diploma score of 39.3 points — well above the world average of 30.58. Some 72 per cent of candidates scored 38 points or more. The school has also been named among the top 100 private schools in the world by Spears for the fifth consecutive year, and among the top 15 in Asia Pacific. For families considering the 2026–27 intake, Tanglin operates an oversubscribed Waitpool system rather than a chronological waitlist, with applications accepted year-round.
